htmlContent:
NC 911 Board Monthly Dispatch

12th March, 2025 2nd Edition

NEW 911 BOARD MEMBERS

Four new NC 911 Board members were sworn in on February 28, 2025 at the February 911 Board Meeting.

Please join us in welcoming new 911 Board Members Jason Compton, Heather Hallmark, Michael James, and Joey Nelson.

NENA 911 Goes To Washington

2/24/2025 - 2/26/2025

9-1-1 Goes to Washington brings together fellow 9-1-1 professionals across the country with government leaders in our nation's capital to address the most pressing issues related to emergency communications. This year while in Washington DC, 9-1-1 Professionals from North Carolina met with Senator Budd, Senator Tillis, and eight (8) representatives from Congress to discuss the 9-1-1 SAVES Act and Next Generation funding for states across the country. It is always an honor to represent our industry and to educate on the progress North Carolina has made in Next Gen services!

In the Field... The Eastern Region Report

In the Field...

The Eastern Region Report

Kristen Falco has been meeting with several PSAPs to work on funding reconsideration requests and review financial planning tools. She visited the PSAPs in Wayne, Carteret, and Onslow Counties. She has also been working on eligibility requests, doing outreach for PSAPs working on CAD-to-CAD projects, and assisting with grant documentation management.

Onslow County is in the middle of an EOC renovation. Telecommunicators continue to answer 911 calls as renovations take place on their building for the expansion of the County's EOC.

In the Field...

The Eastern Region Report

Telecommunicators in Carteret County decorated for Valentine's Day and enjoy working on their new furniture in the updated PSAP layout.

In the Field...

The Eastern Region Report

Telecommunicators in Wayne County decorated for upcoming St. Patrick's Day.

In the Field...(cont.) The North Central Region Report

In the Field...(cont.)

The North Central Region Report

Brian Short has been working with PSAP managers in the North Central Region on the Financial Planning Tools and also assisting with Funding Reconsideration requests. He visited Caswell County's primary and backup PSAPs. Brian also continues to work on eligibility requests and attended the PSAP NowGen 2.0 roadshows in the South Central and Western Regions. Brian also attended the annual NENA 911 Goes to Washington event along with several other Board staff members and Executive Director, Pokey Harris.

In the Field...(cont.) The South Central Region Report

The South Central Region Report

David Newberry worked the EOC on February 12, for Winter Weather ESF2 Coverage.

David reviewed FY26 CRI project submissions from Johnston County 911, Perquimans-Gates 911, and Union County 911. The submissions were presented to the Technology Committee who gave approval on February 13. The NC 911 Board approved the three submissions on February 28, 2025.

On February 24, David participated in the AT&T Leadership Team Tour of the Raleigh-Wake Emergency Communications Center.

Pictured are former NC 911 Board member Chuck Greene, current NC 911 Board member Joey Nelson; AT&T Leadership Caroline Van Wie, Rhonda Johnson, Trey Rabon, Meredith Williams; South Central Regional Coordinator David Newberry

In the Field...(cont.) The Western Region Report

The Western Region Report

Stephanie Conner has been busy working with PSAPs on financial planning tools, funding reconsiderations, and closing out grants! She also participated in the first NowGen2.0 Roadshow in the Western Region!

The Western Region Report

In the Field...(cont.) PSAP Assessment Coordinator Report

PSAP Assessment Coordinator Report

Matt Boyles continues to work on PSAP Assessments throughout the State and the overall program. Matt visited Caswell County, Alexander County and Wilkes County PSAPs with the respective RC's. Matt also attended the NowGen 2.0 Roadshow in Boone. Matt is working on other projects, like CAD-to-CAD initiative proposals and PSAP eligibility request.

A Word from Your RCs...

STATESCOOP 50 Awards Nominations

This year, the 911 Board's Executive Director L.V. Pokey Harris has been nominated for the Golden Gov Award, which is an award for a visionary state executive leading state government into a new technology landscape with innovative ideas and inspiring others to get on board.

Not only was our Executive Director nominated, but our NG911 Project was also nominated for State IT Innovation of the Year as a cutting-edge state IT approach to cross-agency or intra-agency technology that embraces innovation.

You can cast your vote using the link below for both Pokey Harris and the NG911 Project now through March 14, 2025.

Congratulations on the nominations!

https://statescoop.com/statescoop50/vote/#

Western RC Words of Wisdom......

We are here to help! Do not hesitate to reach out if you have any questions or a need!

-Stephanie Conner

Finance Reminders

Talking All Things Money.....

CAD-to-CAD for FY2025 Reimbursement Request Reminder

All CAD-to-CAD reimbursement requests for FY2025 must be submitted for processing to the 911 Board staff via the C2Cproposal@nc.gov email address no later than May 1, 2025. If you believe you will have an issue meeting this deadline, please submit an email to C2Cproposal@nc.gov advising of the issues or concerns.

Have questions on how to submit for reimbursement? Follow the step-by-step instructions on the NG911 CAD-to-CAD Cloud Based Subscription Service document located on the 911 Board's website under the Grants and Documents section, under Forms and Documents.

CRI Reimbursement Reminder

All CRI project reimbursement requests for FY2025 must be submitted for processing to the 911 Board staff via the CRIProposal@nc.gov email address no later than May 1, 2025. If you believe you will have an issue meeting this deadline, please submit an email to CRIproposal@nc.gov advising of the issues or concerns.

Have questions on how to submit for reimbursement? Follow the step-by-step instructions on the PSAP Cybersecurity Remediation Initiative (CRI) Proposal document located on the 911 Board's website under the Grants and Documents section, under Forms and Documents.

Pokey's Post-It

It is so exciting to have received such positive feedback about the first edition newsletter. The second edition continues to provide pertinent and timely information. Be sure to share your "happenings" with us so we can include them in a future edition. Kudos to Kristen and staff for another great Monthly Dispatch!!!

PSAP Highlights

Kevin Anderson with Raleigh-Wake Emergency Communications Retires!

Kevin Anderson retired after 21 years with the Raleigh-Wake Emergency Communications Center. Over the past 21 years, Kevin has shown a dedication to preserving life and property by answering 911 calls for the citizens and first responders of the City of Raleigh and Wake County. He has been an institutional pillar of knowledge within the Raleigh-Wake Emergency Communications Center. He has supervised and trained countless 911 professionals over his 911 career and touched the lives of those he worked with through his words of encouragement, sense of humor, and occasional outbreak into song. He is looking forward to spending all of his newfound free time enjoying the company of family and friends.

Brett Renfrow with Johnston County 911 Retires!

Brett Renfrow retired after 30+ years in the 911 industry from Johnston County 911 Communications as the 911 Director on February 1st. Throughout his career in 911, he saw many changes to 911 technology and even took on a new PSAP Project, overseeing the design and construction process for the new Johnston County 911 Communications Center. He served on the 911 Board's Grant Committee, offering valuable input on PSAP grant applications and presentations. He has been a staunch advocate for 911 telecommunicators and the important work they do over. We wish Brett all the best in his well-deserved retirement!

WANT TO Share WITH US?

We want to hear from you! If you have a story, a topic, or any other ideas for the NC 911 Board Monthly Dispatch, reach out to your Regional Coordinator.

We want the NC 911 Board Monthly Dispatch to be informative about all things 911 Board, and to highlight and celebrate the successes of 911 here in North Carolina!

[Pages 11-21 unavailable]

Content from pages 11-21 could not be converted. This section may be available after retry.
